refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies a solution approach. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the functional goal without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single functional objective: meeting the energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates measurement and control functions, supporting modularity. Coupling risk arises if feedback control depends on external operational policies, which should be managed via clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Energy measurement can be accurately and reliably obtained in real-time
Feedback control algorithms can be tuned to meet diverse customer-defined targets
Operational policies remain stable or are communicated timely to the control system
Energy efficiency targets are achievable given physical and operational constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between energy monitoring, control modules, and operational policy inputs to ensure modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with external systems or components for data exchange and control commands.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Partial containment since energy consumption relates to vehicle operation, but feedback control logic may diffuse outside vehicle module.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not energy monitoring.
Safety may use energy data but does not contain the monitoring and control functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, minimizing diffusion and centralizing related functionality.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid diffusion of control logic outside NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target. The DP logically supports measuring and controlling energy use to meet the acceptance criterion of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid diffusion of control logic outside NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) with minimal diffusion (2), indicating good architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the energy_per_ton metric over the evaluation period as per acceptance criteria, and ensuring the NGHE Solution module provides the necessary data and control feedback.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with ownership candidate explicitly identified and interfaces defined to support implementation.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize the interfaces with the Vehicle module to minimize coupling and ensure clean data acquisition pathways.
Define strict interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to prevent diffusion of control logic outside the NGHE Solution module.
Consider documenting fallback or error handling strategies if data from Vehicle or external interfaces is delayed or unavailable to maintain control robustness.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear logical support, good architectural packaging, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module is well-defined as the implementation module with high cohesion and minimal diffusion. Although some interface clarifications and formalizations are recommended, these are refinements rather than fundamental issues requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable top-level FR is preferable to avoid complexity and potential errors from deeper decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To clarify and formalize interfaces with Vehicle, Telemetry, and Network Link modules to minimize coupling and diffusion, and to document fallback/error handling strategies for robustness.

assumptions: The current DP fully addresses the top-level FR for energy efficiency target achievement.
The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the DP effectively with manageable interface dependencies.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for raw data acquisition.
Risk of control logic diffusion through Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
evidence_needed: Formal interface specifications and contracts with Vehicle, Telemetry, and Network Link modules.
Verification data showing energy_per_ton metric compliance over evaluation period.
Documentation of fallback and error handling strategies for data delays or unavailability.
